# Custom Learning Paths with CUDA-Q Academic

Instead of a fixed syllabus, CUDA-Q Academic gives you a tool to build the path that fits your course — and hand it directly to students as a single link.

**[Open the Learning Path Builder →](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html)**

---

## How It Works

### 1. Pick your modules

The builder shows all available modules. Select any subset — a single module for a workshop, a handful for a unit within a larger course, or everything for a dedicated quantum computing course.

| Module | Level | What it covers |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Quick Start to Quantum Computing | Intro | Single qubits through a first variational algorithm in CUDA-Q |
| QIS Foundational Algorithms | Intro–Intermediate | Teleportation, oracle problems, QFT, phase estimation, Grover, Shor |
| Quantum Algorithm Simulation 101 | Intermediate | State vector, tensor network, MPS, Pauli propagation, and stabilizer simulation tradeoffs |
| QEC 101 | Intermediate–Advanced | Repetition codes, stabilizers, surface codes, qLDPC, AI decoders, real-time decoding |
| Chemistry Simulations | Intermediate–Advanced | VQE, ADAPT-VQE, QPE, Krylov methods, QM/MM workflows |
| Applications to Finance | Intermediate–Advanced | Quantum walks, portfolio optimization, QAOA, Q-CHOP |
| QAOA for Max Cut (Circuit Cutting) | Intermediate | QAOA with divide-and-conquer and recursive circuit-cutting strategies |
| AI for Quantum | Intermediate | AI models for unitary compilation, error correction, and eigensolvers |
| Dynamics 101 | Intermediate | Jaynes-Cummings dynamics and time-dependent Hamiltonians |
| Hybrid Workflows | Intermediate | Cross-cutting hybrid classical-quantum workflows |
| Quantum AI Project Template | Intermediate–Advanced | Role-based group project structure with AI agent configurations |

### 2. Pick the notebooks

Each module opens into a flat list of its notebooks. Keep what's relevant to your course topic and timeline — skip the rest. You don't need to use a complete module.

### 3. Drop in widgets

Alongside notebooks, you can add interactive tools — visualizations like the Bloch sphere explorer, Born's Rule, Hello World circuits — directly into the path. Useful as lecture companions or self-check activities.

### 4. Generate and share the link

That's it. The builder generates a URL that encodes exactly what you selected. Students open one link and land on the path you built — nothing more, nothing less.

---

## Example Paths

These illustrate a few ways the builder can be used. Each example has a ready-made link below — click **Open this path →** to launch the assembled path directly, or open it in the builder to remix it for your own course.

**Intro course unit (1–2 weeks)**
Start with Quick Start to Quantum Computing (all four notebooks), add two or three notebooks from QIS Foundational Algorithms, and drop in the Bloch sphere and Born's Rule widgets. Students arrive with working CUDA-Q code and intuition for the first algorithm examples before any lecture.
[**Open this path →**](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html?)

**Algorithms survey course**
Select all of QIS Foundational Algorithms, VQE from Chemistry Simulations, and the first two notebooks from QAOA for Max Cut. Skip the circuit-cutting extension notebooks if time is short. The path covers teleportation through Shor's with runnable implementations at every step, and adds a variational algorithm example before the QAOA unit.
[**Open this path →**](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html?)

**Graduate quantum error correction unit**
Select QEC 101 in full — nine notebooks from repetition codes through real-time decoding. Add the Simulation 101 notebook as a prerequisite context piece. The path gives students a self-contained arc from first principles to current research topics.
[**Open this path →**](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html?)

**Applied / interdisciplinary course**
Pick Quick Start (notebooks 1–3 as prerequisite background), then select the domain track relevant to your field — finance, chemistry, or AI for quantum — and add the Hybrid Workflows module to show how the pieces connect. End with the Quantum AI Project Template to give students a structure for a final project.
[**Open this path →**](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html?)

**Workshop or short course (half-day to two days)**
Select Quick Start notebooks 1–2, the QAOA for Max Cut and GPT-QAOA notebook introduces quantum computing and CUDA-Q in the context of Max-Cut QAOA through GPT-assisted hybrid workflows, with an optional section for running on IQM hardware. A shareable link means zero setup coordination — students arrive, click the link, and have exactly the right starting point.
[**Open this path →**](https://nvidia.github.io/cuda-q-academic/learningpath.html?)

---

## Tips

- **Match your prerequisites.** The Quick Start track is the entry point for students without prior CUDA-Q experience. Modules labeled intermediate or advanced assume comfort writing and running circuits.
- **The link is permanent.** Once generated, the URL encodes your selections and can be shared via a course LMS, syllabus page, or email. Students don't need accounts.
- **You can make multiple paths.** Build one path for a prerequisite unit, a second for the core module, and a third for an optional extension. Each is a separate link.
- **The Quantum AI Project Template is not a self-paced module.** It is an instructor resource — a role-based group project structure with AI agent configurations and faculty deployment materials. Use it to anchor a final project after students have completed the content modules.

---

*For more on how to deploy the project template, see the [Quantum AI Project Template Teaching Guide](./quantum-ai-project-template/Teaching-Guide.md). Questions or feedback: [cuda-quantum-academic@nvidia.com](mailto:cuda-quantum-academic@nvidia.com).*
